headline: RM15B MRT3 Circle Line Faces 18-Month Delay
context: The RM15 billion MRT Circle Line in Kuala Lumpur encountered an 18-month delay in January 2026 due to tunnelling complications and land acquisition disputes, pushing completion from 2028 to late 2029.

cards.big: MRT3 delayed 18 months — RM15 billion project won't finish until 2029, adding estimated RM2.2 billion in cost overruns
cards.sub: This is the 3rd major KL rail project to exceed budget — MRT1 overran by 12%, MRT2 by 18%

cards.big: Every month of MRT3 delay costs RM120 million in interest on construction financing alone
cards.sub: 18-month delay means RM2.2 billion additional cost — enough to build 4 complete BRT lines in secondary cities

cards.big: 32 businesses along Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman forced to close during extended construction disruption period
cards.sub: Compensation scheme covers only physical damage — not 18 months of lost revenue for shops blocked by hoarding

cards.big: MRT Corp's project management office reports to Finance Ministry — no independent infrastructure oversight body exists
cards.sub: Previous MRT lines had cost monitoring by independent consultants — MRT3 uses internal assessment only

cards.big: KL keeps building expensive rail lines that go over budget — maybe the problem is how Malaysia manages mega-projects, not which projects it picks
Should Malaysia establish an independent infrastructure delivery authority modelled on UK's IPA to oversee all projects above RM1 billion?
